records.ety: [Akin to D. krullen, Dan. krölle, dial. Sw. krulla to curl, crisp; possibly akin to E. crook. Cf. Curl, n., Cruller.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Curled (krld); p. pr. & vb. n. Curling.]

defs: To twist or form into ringlets; to crisp, as the hair.
quotes: But curl their locks with bodkins and with braid.

defs: To twist or make onto coils, as a serpent's body.
quotes: Of his tortuous train, Curled many a wanton wreath in sight of Eve.

defs: To deck with, or as with, curls; to ornament.
quotes: Thicker than the snaky locks That curledMegæra.
Curling with metaphors a plain intention.

defs: To raise in waves or undulations; to ripple.
quotes: Seas would be pools without the brushing air To curl the waves.

defs: To shape (the brim) into a curve.
To contract or bend into curls or ringlets, as hair; to grow in curls or spirals, as a vine; to be crinkled or contorted; to have a curly appearance; as, leaves lie curled on the ground.
quotes: Thou seest it [hair] will not curl by nature.

defs: To move in curves, spirals, or undulations; to contract in curving outlines; to bend in a curved form; to make a curl or curls.
quotes: Then round her slender waist he curled.
Curling smokes from village tops are seen.
Gayly curl the waves before each dashing prow.
He smiled a king of sickly smile, and curled up on the floor.

defs: To play at the game called curling.

records.ety: [Akin to D. krul, Dan. krölle. See Curl, v. ]

defs: A ringlet, especially of hair; anything of a spiral or winding form.
quotes: Under a coronet, his flowing hair In curls on either cheek played.

defs: An undulating or waving line or streak in any substance, as wood, glass, etc.; flexure; sinuosity.
quotes: If the glass of the prisms . . . be without those numberless waves or curls which usually arise from the sand holes.

defs: A disease in potatoes, in which the leaves, at their first appearance, seem curled and shrunken.
